canonical_definition: WikiDatacounterfact is characterized in the provided literature as a dataset used for knowledge editing evaluation. The single available definition states that it contains "counterfactual statements derived from Wikidata." Its most common application is to measure the construct of "Knowledge editing," with some papers specifying its use in experiments on "knowledge insertion and knowledge modification." A less frequent use is also noted, where one paper employs it to measure "Locality." One source also situates the dataset within the broader "KnowEdit (Zhang et al., 2024) benchmark."
papers: In-Context Editing: Learning Knowledge from Self-Induced Distributions
Precise Localization of Memories: A Fine-grained Neuron-level Knowledge Editing Technique for LLMs
Revealing and Mitigating Over-Attention in Knowledge Editing
Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning

definitions.text: A dataset used for knowledge editing evaluation that contains counterfactual statements derived from Wikidata.

relationships.evidence: "We select three datasets including knowledge insertion and knowledge modification in our experiments: WikiDatacounterfact (Cohen et al., 2024), WikiDatarecent (Cohen et al., 2024) and ZsRE (Levy et al., 2017)."
